title: General wedding details
text:
NotePage under construction
Kia ora all,
Our wedding will be held on the 23rd of January, 2027 NZDT. The venue will be our house - 23 Kererū street, Taupō, New Zealand.
This site is to provide you with some details about logistics, and to help everyone stay organised. Specifically to the yanks coming all this way - I appreciate that NZ is a long way to travel for a wedding, and we want to help as much as we can to make it a smooth trip. Hopefully after visiting you will understand why we have chosen to live here.
I hope this can be treated as forced vacation for all of you. The word “forced” meaning that you will feel strongly obliged to take some time of work, and treat yourself to a vacation in this beautiful country. I understand this is an expensive trip, and we appreciate you coming very much.

title: General wedding details
text:
On the day information

Venue
The ceremony will be held at out house - 23 Kererū street. It’s the mansion at the end of the street. Actually it’s not a mansion, but we should all fit. After the ceremony there will be a lot of mingling on our feet, so keep that in mind when picking shoes.


parking and travel
There is plenty of on street parking on our street. Drive, walk, bike, whatever you want. You will only need to organise your travel home at the end of the night because the reception is only a short walk from our house.


Dress Code
This is a lowkey event, but still a formal one. Dress in your best clothes, whatever that may be 😃. It’s a summer garden wedding, so leave the tuxedos at home and find some colours.


Wedding size
This is a small event with only our closest friends and family invited. Please no plus-ones or children unless specified. Exclusive, chic, fashionable 💅.

title: General wedding details
text:
Other Information

Accommodation Options
Taupō has many places to stay. We do not have any specific recommendations, but do recommendation you use for following sites for your search:
Holiday homes
Airbnb
BookaBatch
Because of the volume of accommodation options you can be choosy. We recommend getting something within a few km’s of our house.


Getting to NZ





There are a 3 or so airlines that fly to New Zealand direct from U.S.A. We have flown on a few of them, and from those experiences, recommend Air New Zealand. AirNZ has nice planes, service, food, and offer a great option those with babies of a thing called the “sky couch”. Air New Zealand is also a member of the Star Alliance so you can use points you have accumulated from other Star Alliance airlines (such as United airlines).
AirNZ flies direct to Auckland NZ from Los Angles, San Francisco, New York, Vancouver, and Houston. From Auckland you can fly to Taupō (a 50 min. flight). The flight from Auckland to Taupō is less than $80 USD so that probably makes the most sense. We encourage you to send us your flights before you book them, sometimes the slightly cheaper option will send you the wrong way around the world and be much worse.

title: General wedding details
text:
Things to do in Taupō
Taupō is a small town in the centre of the north island of NZ. We lived in Dunedin, Wellington, and have now landed here. Taupō is known for it’s natural beauty, big ass lake, tourist attractions, and proximity to the mountain. The natural beauty highlights include geothermal pools, the maunga Ruapehū, and swimming spots. Although there are many tourists, it still feels like a small town. Lake Taupō is the biggest lake in NZ, and feels a lot like lake Michigan in terms of its clarity and summer temperature.
Because of the tourist industry there is lots to do in Taupō. We have bungy jumping, sky diving, river jet boat rides, fly fishing, wine tasting, beaches.. the list is endless really. We’ve provided some recommendations below to at least give you a place to start.






Whakamoenga Point Rock Carvings


To eat and drink

Lionel’s


👍

Food is excellent,
vibe is excellent,
covered seating,
Our favourite restaurant in town.

👎

None. Go there.




Two Mile Bay Sailing Club


👍

Locals favourite,
in Two Mile Bay,
little ones (and Zach) can swim while adults drink,

👎

Only sell one beer,
only do pizza




Thai Delight


👍

Centre of town,
good food,
really nice staff.

👎

Won’t blow your socks off. But will be good.




Ploughmans Restaurant


👍

Nice atmosphere for a drink,
outdoor seating,
next to “Fish Box” fish-and-chips,
in Two Mile Bay

👎

grumpy waitress,
cannot vouch for food.





To do

our favourite swimming spots

Whakaipo bay
Whakamoenga Point

park at Rangatira Point car park
Follow the track west until it ends. You are there.

Kinloch beach

General store is cute and has good ice cream.

Town beach



cycling

You can rent bikes from lots of places. “Pack & Pedal” is our local bike shop so I recommend them.
Cycle the W2K trail.

Bike about 26 km round trip from Whakaipo to Kinloch. Great bush and lake views. See this link for more information.

Cycle along the lakefront. A nice cruizey in-town ride. Can still make it over 25 kms if you do the entire thing.



Hot pools/regular pools

Wairakei terraces

A few minutes out of town. Our favourite.

Debretts.

Got waterslides ’n stuff.

AC Baths

Local gym and outdoor pool we like.



Ngā mihi,
John and Ella
